## 2026-05-24 02:07 KST - MBRControl default Get repair and DataRemoval reserved enum expansion

- Change type: solver repair + sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden MBRControl readback/default-state tests. Before this repair, successful MBRControl `Get` responses were accepted without checking returned `Enable`, `Done`, or `DoneOnReset` cells.
  - Low for DataRemoval reserved enum tests; existing enum validation already rejected all reserved values, but the trusted corpus now covers more of the reserved range.
- Trigger:
  - `opal/4.3.5.3` preconfigures MBRControl with `Enable=false`, `Done=false`, and `DoneOnReset=PowerCycle`.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.6` says successful `Set` applies all parameterized changes.
  - `opal/3.1.1.6.4` and `opal/4.2.5.1` mark data-removal mechanism values `3-4` and `6-7` reserved.
  - `opal/4.2.6.1.2` says unsupported `ActiveDataRemovalMechanism` values fail `Set` with `INVALID_PARAMETER`.
- Result:
  - Added 8 accepted `mbrcontrol-defaults-doc` cases.
  - Added 6 accepted `data-removal-reserved-doc` cases.
  - Sourced corpus is now 408 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 396 cases.
  - Referenced official sections increased from 279 to 282.
- Solver changes:
  - `src/solver_components/expectations.py::_expected_get` now attaches expected MBRControl return cells when a successful `Get` reports known cells.
  - `src/solver_components/engine.py::_return_cell_matches` now compares MBRControl `DoneOnReset` as a reset-type set, so symbolic values like `PowerCycle` and numeric values compare correctly.
  - Existing MBRControl `Set` transition tracking is reused so later `Get` expectations reflect successful Admins updates.
  - No production solver repair was needed for DataRemoval reserved values.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers agreed on all 14 new labels, with no concerns.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 589 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag mbrcontrol-defaults-doc`: 8 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag data-removal-reserved-doc`: 6 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 408 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 408 cases, 1287 review rows, 396 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 396 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.

## 2026-05-24 02:39 KST - Issued Authority identity-column read-only repair

- Change type: solver fix + sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden Authority table write tests. Before this repair, issued Authority `CommonName` could be accepted as a successful host `Set`, even though the Core Authority table makes issued `CommonName` non-host-modifiable.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.2.10` says Authority `UID` is not host-modifiable and issued Authority `Name`/`CommonName` are not host-modifiable.
  - `opal/4.2.1.7` and `opal/4.3.1.8` provide concrete Admin SP and Locking SP issued Authority rows.
- Result:
  - Added 21 accepted `authority-issued-readonly-doc` cases.
  - Sourced corpus is now 474 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 462 cases.
  - Referenced official sections stayed at 291 because the Authority sources were already referenced by earlier auth/session cases.
- Mismatch summary:
  - case family: `authority-issued-readonly-doc` `CommonName` host Set cases.
  - concepts: `auth`, `authority`, `issued-row`, `read-only-column`, `set`.
  - solver behavior before repair: allowed `SUCCESS` for `Set` on issued Authority `CommonName` column `0x02`.
  - doc label: `SUCCESS` is impossible for issued Authority `CommonName`.
- Solver changes:
  - `src/solver_components/semantics.py::_invalid_set_values` now treats issued Authority `CommonName` as read-only, alongside existing rejection of Authority `UID`/`Name`.
  - Added helper logic to identify issued Authority symbols such as `Authority_Anybody`, `Authority_Admins`, `Authority_SID`, `Authority_Users`, `Authority_AdminN`, and `Authority_UserN`.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers agreed on all 21 labels, with no concerns.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 593 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag authority-issued-readonly-doc`: 21 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 474 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 474 cases, 1485 review rows, 462 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 462 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 474 cases, 291 covered docs, 360 untriaged A/B.

## 2026-05-24 03:36 KST - AccessControl meta-method parameter and result-shape sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden `GetACL` result-shape tests. The model previously accepted a successful `GetACL` response whose return payload was a scalar ACE reference; official Core text requires a list of ACE uidrefs.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.3.11` through `core/5.3.3.11.3.1` define `DeleteMethod` on `AccessControlTableUID`, requiring `InvokingID`/`MethodID` and returning an empty list on success.
  - `core/5.3.3.13` through `core/5.3.3.13.3.1` define `GetACL` on `AccessControlTableUID`, requiring `InvokingID`/`MethodID` and returning a list of ACE uidrefs.
  - `core/5.3.3.14` and `core/5.3.3.15` define `AddACE`/`RemoveACE` with required `InvokingID`, `MethodID`, and `ACE`, and empty success results.
- Result:
  - Added 18 accepted `acl-method-params-doc` cases.
  - Sourced corpus is now 677 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 665 cases.
  - Referenced official sections increased from 344 to 355.
- Solver changes:
  - `_expected_get_acl` now marks successful `GetACL` responses with `expected_return_uid_list=True`, so scalar ACE references or non-UID-list payloads fail.
  - Added a unit regression for scalar `GetACL` success return rejection.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers agreed on all 18 labels, with no concerns.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 595 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag acl-method-params-doc`: 18 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 677 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 677 cases, 2094 review rows, 665 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 665 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.

## 2026-05-24 03:49 KST - Authenticate result boolean sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium to high for hidden authentication-result-shape tests. The model previously accepted some `Authenticate` `SUCCESS` responses with omitted result booleans, and accepted `Anybody` `SUCCESS False`.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.3.12` defines `Authenticate` as `ThisSP.Authenticate` returning `Result : typeOr { Success : boolean, Challenge : bytes }`.
  - `core/5.3.4.1.14` and `core/5.3.4.1.14.1` state that Password authentication returns True on success and False on failure; Anybody Authenticate always succeeds when syntax is otherwise correct.
  - `core/5.3.3.7`, `core/5.3.4.2.6`, and `core/5.3.2.12.4` bind a prior successful C_PIN `Set` to the later SID `Proof` value.
  - `opal/4.2.1.7` gives Anybody concrete UID `0000000900000001`.
- Result:
  - Added 7 accepted `authenticate-result-doc` cases after two rework rounds to remove reviewer concerns.
  - Sourced corpus is now 684 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 672 cases.
  - Referenced official sections increased from 355 to 361.
- Solver changes:
  - `Authenticate` parsing now accepts `Proof`/`proof` as a credential input alongside existing API-friendly challenge fields.
  - Expected-response comparison now fails when `expected_return_bool` is set and a successful response omits a boolean result.
  - `_expected_authenticate` now requires `SUCCESS True` for known-correct Password and Anybody authentication, and `SUCCESS False` for known incorrect Password authentication when the status is successful.
- Review note:
  - First review round quarantined SID cases because the trajectory used `Challenge` instead of the source's `Proof`.
  - Second review round quarantined cases where the packet did not quote prior `Set`/PIN semantics or used symbolic Anybody instead of the concrete UID.
  - Final review round accepted all 7 cases with three independent agreeing reviewers and no concerns.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 599 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag authenticate-result-doc`: 7 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 684 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 684 cases, 2157 review rows, 672 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 672 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 684 cases, 361 covered docs, 330 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 361 covered, 284 pending A/B cartography.

## 2026-05-24 11:47 KST - C_PIN TryLimit observable Tries state sourced batch

- Change type: long stateful sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium to high for hidden C_PIN state-observation tests. The model already tracked failed authentication and reset effects internally, but it accepted any successful `C_PIN_User1.Get` payload for non-PIN cells. Hidden tests that ask whether `Tries`/`TryLimit` values are correctly reflected after failed `Authenticate`, successful auth, PIN `Set`, or reset events should now be more likely to pass.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.4.1.1.2` defines `TryLimit`/`Tries` behavior: failed authentication increments `Tries` when `TryLimit` is nonzero, `TryLimit=0` keeps `Tries` at 0, successful authentication and PIN modification reset `Tries`, `PowerCycle` resets nonpersistent `Tries`, and Hardware Reset does not.
  - `opal/4.3.1.9` gives the concrete Locking SP C_PIN rows and exposes `TryLimit`, `Tries`, and `Persistence`.
- Result:
  - Added 14 accepted `trylimit-tries-get-doc` cases.
  - Sourced corpus is now 698 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 686 cases.
  - Referenced official sections remain 361 because this batch deepened an already-covered C_PIN slice rather than adding new source files.
- Solver changes:
  - Added `_cpin_expected_cells` and wired C_PIN non-PIN `Get` expectations to validate known `TryLimit`, `Tries`, and `Persistence` return cells.
  - Added unit regressions for failed `Authenticate` updating observable `Tries`, wrong `Tries` payload rejection, and `PowerCycle` reset of nonpersistent `Tries`.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers agreed on all 14 labels, with no concerns.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 602 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag trylimit-tries-get-doc`: 14 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 698 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 698 cases, 2199 review rows, 686 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 686 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 698 cases, 361 covered docs, 330 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 361 covered, 284 pending A/B cartography.

## 2026-05-24 12:21 KST - Authority and Locking observable state sourced batches

- Change type: long stateful sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - High for hidden state-observation tests that submit successful `Get` responses after prior `Set`, authentication, or reset events. The model previously tracked some state internally but accepted stale or arbitrary `Get` payloads for `Authority.Uses`, Locking range geometry cells, and `LockOnReset`.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.2.10` defines Authority `Limit`/`Uses` as the count and cap for successful explicit or implicit authentications.
  - `core/5.7.2.2.4` through `core/5.7.2.2.10` define Locking `RangeStart`, `RangeLength`, lock-enable, lock-state, and `LockOnReset` cells.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.2` and `core/5.3.4.2.6` bind successful `Set` changes to later readable `Get` cell values.
  - `opal/4.3.5.2` preconfigures Locking range defaults, including `LockOnReset=Power Cycle`; `opal/4.3.5.2.2` requires support for `{0}` and `{0,3}` reset-type sets.
- Result:
  - Added 12 accepted `authority-uses-get-doc` cases.
  - Added 8 accepted `lockonreset-get-doc` cases.
  - Added 12 accepted `locking-range-get-doc` cases.
  - Added 8 accepted `locking-lockonreset-get-doc` cases.
  - Added 8 pending-review `locking-composite-get-doc` cases for multi-column Cellblock trajectories.
  - Sourced corpus is now 746 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 726 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Authority `Get` now validates tracked `Limit` and `Uses` cells.
  - Locking `Get` now validates tracked `RangeStart`, `RangeLength`, `ReadLockEnabled`, `WriteLockEnabled`, `ReadLocked`, `WriteLocked`, and `LockOnReset`.
  - New RangeState defaults reflect Opal Locking row preconfiguration for `LockOnReset={0}`.
  - Return-cell comparison now treats Locking column 9 as a `reset_types` set rather than a plain scalar/string.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers agreed on `authority-uses-get-doc`, `lockonreset-get-doc`, `locking-range-get-doc`, and `locking-lockonreset-get-doc` labels, with no concerns.
  - `locking-composite-get-doc` review is in progress and remains outside the consensus gate until three agreeing no-concern reviews land.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 610 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag authority-uses-get-doc`: 12 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag lockonreset-get-doc`: 8 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag locking-range-get-doc`: 12 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag locking-lockonreset-get-doc`: 8 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag locking-composite-get-doc`: 8 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 746 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 746 cases, 2319 review rows, 726 accepted, 20 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 706 accepted cases before the latest report, 0 mismatches; rerun after composite review closes.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.

## 2026-05-24 12:51 KST - DataStore payload and personalized ACE byte-table Get semantics

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + wrapper regressions + continuous-loop runner
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - High for hidden DataStore/readData/writeData/readAccess/writeAccess tests. The model now tracks written DataStore bytes through both direct method records and TCGstorageAPI wrapper calls, and it handles the official byte-table unauthorized-Get rule as `SUCCESS` with an empty result rather than `NOT_AUTHORIZED`.
- Trigger:
  - `opal/4.3.8.1` defines DataStore as a Locking SP byte table whose access control initially requires Admins and is personalizable.
  - `core/5.3.4.3.3` permits modifying ACE `BooleanExpr` through `Set`.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.2` states that if authenticated authorities do not satisfy access control for invoking `Get` on a byte table, the method returns an empty results list.
  - `core/5.3.3.7.2.1` and `core/5.3.3.6.2.1` define byte-table `Set(Bytes)` and `Get(Bytes)` payload behavior.
- Result:
  - Added 6 accepted `datastore-payload-doc` cases covering Admin1 DataStore byte payload provenance and overwrite behavior.
  - Added 8 accepted `datastore-user-payload-doc` cases covering User1 DataStore Get/Set ACE personalization, payload provenance, and unauthorized byte-table Get empty-result behavior.
  - Added `tools/continuous_edge_loop.py` so consensus, gate, sourced, synthetic, unit, public eval, coverage, and inventory checks can be rerun as a logged loop.
- Solver changes:
  - Added tracked `State.datastore_pattern` and `ExpectedResponse.expected_return_pattern`.
  - Successful DataStore byte-table `Set` now records the written byte payload.
  - Authorized DataStore `Get` now validates the returned payload against the tracked byte pattern when known.
  - Unauthorized DataStore byte-table `Get` now expects `SUCCESS` with an empty result list instead of a method-level `NOT_AUTHORIZED`.
  - Return-pattern extraction now handles common byte payload wrappers and Python bytes literal strings used by local traces.
- Review note:
  - Initial raw bytes-literal review raised a notation concern, so the cases were rewritten to hex payload strings and stale review files were archived under `analysis/label_reviews/superseded/2026-05-24-datastore-raw-bytes/`.
  - The first personalized User1 review found the important rule correction: byte-table unauthorized `Get` returns empty results. The cases and solver were updated, then three independent reviewers accepted all 8 revised labels with no concerns.
- Wrapper regressions:
  - Added tests for high-level `writeAccess` + `readAccess` + `writeData` + `readData` preserving payloads.
  - Added tests that high-level `readData` without `readAccess` accepts `None`/empty and rejects protected payload leakage.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 617 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag datastore-payload-doc`: 6 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag datastore-user-payload-doc`: 8 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 760 cases, 2409 review rows, 748 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 748 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 760 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/continuous_edge_loop.py --iterations 1 --include-slow`: overall ok, public eval `score=100.00`.

## 2026-05-24 13:03 KST - Opal byte-table descriptor Rows and Kind sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden table-descriptor `Get` result validation. The model previously accepted arbitrary successful descriptor payloads for Opal MBR/DataStore table rows and did not map named `Rows`/`Kind` return keys for `Table_*` descriptor objects.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.2.3` defines Table descriptor columns, including `Kind` at column `0x04` and `Rows` at column `0x07`.
  - `opal/4.3.1.3` preconfigures the Locking SP `Table_MBR` and `Table_DataStore` rows as Byte table descriptors.
  - `opal/4.3.5.4` and `opal/4.3.8.1` give the MBR and DataStore minimum byte-table sizes/row counts.
- Result:
  - Added 12 accepted `byte-table-descriptor-rows-doc` cases covering MBR/DataStore `Kind=Byte`, DataStore `Rows >= 0x00A00000`, MBR `Rows >= 0x08000000`, and composite `Kind`+`Rows` `Get` payloads.
  - Sourced corpus is now 772 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 760 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Added `ExpectedResponse.expected_return_min_cells` and return-cell lower-bound comparison.
  - Added `Table_MBR`/`Table_DataStore` `Get` expectations for Byte kind and minimum Rows values.
  - Added Table descriptor named-column parsing for `Kind`, `Rows`, `RowsFree`, `RowBytes`, `MinSize`, and related descriptor fields.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 12 labels with no concerns.
- Verification:

## 2026-05-24 13:15 KST - Opal byte-table mandatory granularity sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium to high for hidden DataStore/MBR byte-table write-alignment and Table descriptor readback tests. The model now uses observed Table descriptor mandatory granularity to judge later byte-table `Set` operations instead of treating any row-addressed byte payload as aligned.
- Trigger:
  - `opal/5.3.1` adds Table descriptor columns `0x0D MandatoryWriteGranularity` and `0x0E RecommendedAccessGranularity`.
  - `opal/5.3.1.1.1` and `opal/5.3.1.2.1` require object-table descriptor rows to report zero for both granularity columns.
  - `opal/5.3.1.1.2` requires byte-table MandatoryWriteGranularity to be no greater than RecommendedAccessGranularity and no greater than `8192`, and requires byte-table `Set` to fail with `INVALID_PARAMETER` when start offset or byte length is not aligned.
  - `core/5.3.3.7.1.2` and `core/5.3.3.7.2.1` bind byte-table `Set` to row offsets and byte payloads.
- Result:
  - Added 16 accepted `byte-table-granularity-doc` cases covering object-table zero granularity, host read-only granularity descriptor columns, byte-table granularity bounds, mandatory-vs-recommended ordering, and DataStore `Set` alignment after an observed descriptor `Get`.
  - Sourced corpus is now 788 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 776 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Added `ExpectedResponse.expected_return_max_cells` and `expected_return_cell_lte` to validate descriptor-cell upper bounds and cross-cell ordering.
  - Added Table descriptor named-column parsing for `MandatoryWriteGranularity` and `RecommendedAccessGranularity`.
  - Successful `Get` on `Table_MBR`/`Table_DataStore` records observed byte-table mandatory/recommended granularity in state.
  - Byte-table `Set` now rejects start offsets or payload lengths that are not aligned to an observed mandatory granularity.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 16 labels with no concerns.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 626 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag byte-table-granularity-doc`: 16 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 788 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 788 cases, 2493 review rows, 776 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 776 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 788 cases, 372 covered docs, 327 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 372 covered, 281 pending A/B cartography.

## 2026-05-24 17:05 KST - CreateTable row allocation sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden `CreateTable` result validation. The model now connects the successful `Rows` result to `MinSize` and `MaxSize` instead of checking only the two-field result shape.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.3.2` defines `CreateTable` as returning `UID` and `Rows`.
  - `core/5.3.3.2.5` defines `MinSize` as the initial row allocation.
  - `core/5.3.3.2.6` requires byte-table `MaxSize` to fail and guarantees a table never exceeds `MaxSize`.
  - `core/5.3.3.2.7` requires byte-table `HintSize` to fail, while object-table `HintSize` remains only a non-guaranteed request.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.1` binds allocated rows to the Table descriptor `Rows` value and states that `HintSize` is not guaranteed.
- Result:
  - Added 14 accepted `create-table-row-allocation-doc` cases covering `Rows >= MinSize`, `Rows <= MaxSize`, positional and named `Rows` result forms, `MaxSize < MinSize`, byte-table `MaxSize`/`HintSize` rejection, and byte-table empty `Columns` requirements.
  - One draft candidate for object-table `HintSize < MinSize` was removed after all reviewers flagged that the provided source text does not prove a required failure status. The model was correspondingly relaxed to avoid overfitting that ambiguous condition.
  - Sourced corpus is now 802 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 790 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Added `ExpectedResponse.expected_return_min_values` and `expected_return_max_values` for method-result values that are not table cells.
  - Added generic return-value selector matching for named payloads such as `Rows` and positional result lists such as `[UID, Rows]`.
  - Successful `CreateTable` now rejects `Rows < MinSize` and `Rows > MaxSize` when `MaxSize` is supplied.
  - `CreateTable` `HintSize` validation now treats it as an unsigned suggestion, not as proof of invalidity solely because it is lower than `MinSize`.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 14 retained labels with no concerns.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 631 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag create-table-row-allocation-doc`: 14 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 802 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 802 cases, 2538 review rows, 790 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 790 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 802 cases, 376 covered docs, 323 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 376 covered, 277 pending A/B cartography.

## 2026-05-24 17:24 KST - CreateRow unique-column sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium to high for hidden host-created table workflows. The model now tracks a created table's declared columns and unique-column set, then validates later `CreateRow` calls instead of treating any non-empty row data as valid.
- Trigger:
  - `core/3.2.5.4` defines unique column value combinations, including multi-column uniqueness.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.1` defines the two `CreateTable.Columns` forms, including the unique/non-unique column split.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.3` requires `CreateRow` to supply values for each row column and fail on duplicate unique combinations.
  - `core/5.3.3.4.2.1` requires successful `CreateRow` to return a list of created row UIDs.
  - `core/5.3.3.4.3` lists failure conditions for full tables, duplicate unique combinations, undeclared columns, and allocation failures.
- Result:
  - Added 10 accepted `create-row-unique-columns-doc` cases covering first-row success, single-column unique conflict, non-unique duplicate allowance, multi-column unique combinations, simple no-unique table duplicates, missing declared column rejection, undeclared column rejection, and one-row UID-list result shape.
  - Sourced corpus is now 812 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 800 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Added created-table schema state: declared columns, unique columns, and observed row values.
  - Successful `CreateTable` now records the host-created object table schema from `Columns`.
  - Successful `CreateRow` on created object tables now records row values.
  - `CreateRow` now rejects row data that omits declared columns, includes undeclared columns, or duplicates an existing unique-column value combination.
  - Added exact UID-list length checking so one-row `CreateRow` cannot return zero or two created row UIDs.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 10 labels with no concerns.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 636 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag create-row-unique-columns-doc`: 10 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 812 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 812 cases, 2568 review rows, 800 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 800 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 812 cases, 378 covered docs, 322 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 378 covered, 276 pending A/B cartography.

## 2026-05-24 17:54 KST - Table descriptor MinSize/MaxSize Set sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden host-created table workflows. The model now distinguishes `Table.Set` on a descriptor row from illegal direct table-object `Set`, and carries created-table size metadata across later `Set`/`CreateRow` operations.
- Trigger:
  - `core/3.2.5.3` defines the UID relationship between an actual table UID and its Table-table descriptor row UID.
  - `core/5.3.2.3.12` says `MinSize` is user-settable, access-control permitting, and the table can contain at least that many rows.
  - `core/5.3.2.3.13` says `MaxSize` is user-settable, may be rejected by the TPer, and `0` means no host-defined row limit.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.1` states `MinSize` cannot be lowered below the recorded value, and `MaxSize` below `MinSize` or current table size must fail with `INVALID_PARAMETER`.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.3` lets later `CreateRow` calls increase the current object-table row count used by the `MaxSize` check.
- Result:
  - Added 13 accepted `table-descriptor-size-set-doc` cases covering descriptor-row `Table.Set` through `Where`, `MaxSize=0`, same-Set `MinSize`/`MaxSize` interactions, lowered `MinSize`, `MaxSize < MinSize`, `MaxSize < current Rows`, and a later `MaxSize` failure after a successful `MinSize` raise.
  - Sourced corpus is now 825 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 813 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Added created-table descriptor UID, MinSize, MaxSize, and allocated-row tracking to `State`.
  - Successful `CreateTable` now records the generated descriptor UID from the returned table UID and records initial size metadata.
  - Successful `CreateRow` on created object tables updates the current allocated-row count.
  - `Table.Set` on a Table descriptor row now routes through `_expected_table_descriptor_size_set` and accepts only sourced `MinSize`/`MaxSize` behavior instead of treating all Table symbols as blanket read-only.
  - Successful descriptor `MinSize`/`MaxSize` `Set` updates state, so later checks use the raised `MinSize`.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 13 labels with no concerns.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 642 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag table-descriptor-size-set-doc`: 13 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 825 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 825 cases, 2607 review rows, 813 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 813 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 825 cases, 381 covered docs, 321 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 381 covered, 275 pending A/B cartography.

## 2026-05-24 18:18 KST - Created table DeleteRow lifecycle sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden host-created table state traces. The model now removes deleted created-table rows from unique-column tracking instead of treating deleted row values as permanently present.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.3.4.2.1` says `CreateRow` returns the UID values assigned to created rows.
  - `core/5.3.3.5` and `core/5.3.3.5.1` define `DeleteRow` and its required `Rows` uidref list.
  - `core/5.3.3.5.2.1` says successful `DeleteRow` returns an empty result list and deletes the object or fails atomically.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.3` says unique-column conflicts are checked against rows of that table.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.4` says successful row deletion deletes the object and associated objects.
- Result:
  - Added 5 accepted `delete-row-created-table-doc` cases covering unique-value reuse after deleting the matching row, preserving unique conflict when a different row was deleted, multi-row deletion, empty result shape, and impossible success when `Rows` is omitted.
  - One draft exact-status case for omitted `Rows -> INVALID_PARAMETER` was removed after two reviewers correctly noted that the evidence proves missing `Rows` is invalid but does not prove the exact status code.
  - Sourced corpus is now 830 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 818 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Added `created_table_row_values_by_uid` to map `CreateRow` result UIDs back to tracked row values.
  - Successful `CreateRow` records row values by returned row UID.
  - Successful `DeleteRow` on host-created object tables removes those row values from unique-conflict state and updates the current row count.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted the 5 retained labels with no concerns. The removed exact-status draft stayed out of the trusted corpus.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 643 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag delete-row-created-table-doc`: 5 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 830 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 830 cases, 2625 review rows, 818 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 818 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 830 cases, 382 covered docs, 320 untriaged A/B.

## 2026-05-24 19:26 KST - Locking reset state machine sourced batch

- Change type: long stateful sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium to high for hidden Locking reset tests. This is directly aligned with the observed 85 -> 87.5 jump hypothesis: the model is now regression-tested on `Set -> reset -> Get` trajectories rather than only `Set -> Get` snapshots.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.7.2.2.6` through `core/5.7.2.2.10` define ReadLockEnabled, WriteLockEnabled, ReadLocked, WriteLocked, and LockOnReset cells.
  - `core/5.7.3.1.1` and `core/5.7.3.1.2` define the Locking reset state machine and reset recovery behavior.
  - `core/3.3.7.1.5` states that hardware resets and power cycles abort all open sessions.
  - `opal/3.2.3` states that an enabled TPER_RESET aborts all open sessions and applies Programmatic LockOnReset to ReadLocked/WriteLocked.
  - `opal/4.2.3.1` defines ProgrammaticResetEnable as the TPER_RESET enable switch.
  - `opal/3.3.5.2` and `opal/4.3.5.2.2` define reset_type values and Opal-supported LockOnReset sets.
- Result:
  - Added 13 accepted `locking-reset-state-machine-doc` cases.
  - Cases cover HardwareReset matching `{0,1}`, HardwareReset not matching `{0,3}`, enabled TPER_RESET/Programmatic matching `{0,3}`, empty LockOnReset preserving prior locked/unlocked cells, disabled lock features being disregarded for host write, and PowerCycle aborting an open Locking session.
  - Sourced corpus is now 843 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 831 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - No production solver change was required. Existing `_apply_reset_event`, reset-type parsing, host reset expectation, and Locking Get return-cell comparison already matched the added official-doc trajectories.
  - Added 4 unit regressions in `tests/test_solver_rules.py` for HardwareReset match/nonmatch, TPER_RESET match, and PowerCycle session abort.
- Review note:
  - The first review packet quarantined 3 cases due to evidence packaging concerns around TPER_RESET naming and PowerCycle session abort.
  - Stale reviews were archived under `analysis/label_reviews/superseded/2026-05-24-locking-reset-evidence-refresh/`.
  - After adding `opal/3.2.3`, `opal/4.2.3.1`, and `core/3.3.7.1.5` to the evidence packet, three independent reviewers accepted all 13 labels with no concerns.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 647 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag locking-reset-state-machine-doc`: 13 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 843 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 843 cases, 2664 review rows, 831 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 831 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 843 cases, 387 covered docs, 316 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 387 covered, 270 pending A/B cartography.

## 2026-05-24 19:45 KST - DataStore offset payload sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium to high for hidden DataStore byte-table traces. The model previously tracked only the most recent whole payload string, so offset writes and subrange reads could be accepted incorrectly.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.3.7.1.2` says byte-table `Set` `Where.Row` is the byte address where modification begins, and omitted `Where` starts at the first row.
  - `core/5.3.3.7.2.1` says byte-table `Set` uses `Bytes` and modifies beginning at the `Where` byte address.
  - `core/5.3.3.6.1` and `core/5.3.3.6.2.1` define `Get` Cellblock scope and byte-table `Bytes` returns from lowest row to highest row.
  - `opal/4.3.8.1` identifies DataStore as a Locking SP byte table.
- Result:
  - Added 8 accepted `datastore-offset-payload-doc` cases covering byte-table subrange `Get`, nonzero-offset `Set`, and partial overwrite behavior.
  - Sourced corpus is now 851 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 839 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Added `State.datastore_bytes` for per-offset DataStore byte tracking.
  - Added `_byte_table_get_range` to parse byte-table `Get` startRow/endRow range arguments.
  - Successful DataStore `Set` now applies known hex payload bytes at the parsed `Where.Row` offset.
  - Authorized DataStore `Get` now derives expected payload from the requested row interval when those bytes are known.
  - Existing broad `datastore_pattern` tracking remains as a fallback for cases that do not expose enough range information.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 8 labels with no concerns.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 651 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag datastore-offset-payload-doc`: 8 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 851 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 851 cases, 2688 review rows, 839 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 839 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 851 cases, 387 covered docs, 316 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 387 covered, 270 pending A/B cartography.

## 2026-05-24 19:57 KST - Locking RevertSP AccessControl meta-ACL sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden AccessControl/GetACL association tests. This repairs a real over-permissive path: Locking SP `ThisSP/RevertSP` was treated like a generic Admin-editable association, but the Opal preconfiguration gives it `GetACLACL=ACE_Admin` and empty Add/Remove/Delete meta-ACL columns.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.4.3` defines AccessControl rows as `InvokingID`/`MethodID` associations with `ACL` and row-specific meta-ACL columns.
  - `core/5.3.4.3.1` defines AddACE, RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod as meta-methods governed by `AddACEACL`, `RemoveACEACL`, `GetACLACL`, and `DeleteMethodACL`.
  - `core/5.1.5.2` states failed authorization for these meta-methods reports `NOT_AUTHORIZED`.
  - `opal/4.3.1.6` preconfigures the Locking SP `ThisSP/RevertSP` row with `GetACLACL=ACE_Admin` and empty AddACEACL/RemoveACEACL/DeleteMethodACL.
- Result:
  - Added 9 accepted `acl-revertsp-meta-doc` cases.
  - Cases cover unauthenticated `GetACL` NOT_AUTHORIZED, impossible unauthenticated `GetACL` success, Admin `GetACL` success result shape, and Admin AddACE/RemoveACE/DeleteMethod NOT_AUTHORIZED vs impossible success when the corresponding meta-ACL column is empty.
  - Sourced corpus is now 860 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 848 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Extended `src/solver_components/expectations.py::_known_meta_acl_authorization` with the Locking SP `ThisSP/RevertSP` row-specific meta-ACL values.
  - Because `_expected_get_acl`, `_expected_acl_mutation`, and `_expected_delete_method` already consult that helper, the fix applies to all four AccessControl meta-method families rather than only the new cases.
  - Added 4 unit regressions in `tests/test_solver_rules.py` for unauthenticated GetACL, impossible unauthenticated success, and empty AddACE/DeleteMethod meta-ACL success rejection.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 9 labels with no concerns.
  - Reviewer files: `analysis/label_reviews/acl_revertsp_{a,b,c}.jsonl`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 655 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag acl-revertsp-meta-doc`: 9 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 860 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 860 cases, 2715 review rows, 848 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 848 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 860 cases, 388 covered docs, 315 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 388 covered, 269 pending A/B cartography.

## 2026-05-24 20:19 KST - C_PIN User1 ACE BooleanExpr sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden ACL/ACE personalization traces. This adds long `Admin personalize ACE -> end session -> User1 session -> C_PIN.Set` trajectories where the outcome depends on BooleanExpr evaluation rather than a simple Admin/default rule.
- Trigger:
  - `core/3.4.2.1` states authenticated authorities evaluate True in ACE Boolean expressions and unauthenticated authorities evaluate False.
  - `core/5.3.2.9` and `core/5.3.2.9.4` define the ACE table and BooleanExpr column.
  - `core/5.3.4.3.3` states the ACE BooleanExpr column is modifiable through `Set`.
  - `opal/4.3.1.6` binds Locking SP `C_PIN_User1/Set` to `ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN`.
  - `opal/4.3.1.7` requires support for the `Admins` and `Admins OR User1` BooleanExpr values for that ACE row.
- Result:
  - Added 5 accepted `cpin-user-ace-booleanexpr-doc` cases.
  - Cases cover successful personalization to `Admins`, successful personalization to `Admins OR User1`, Admins-only blocking a User1 PIN change with `NOT_AUTHORIZED`, impossible User1 success under Admins-only, and successful User1 PIN change under `Admins OR User1`.
  - Sourced corpus is now 865 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 853 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - No production solver change was required. Existing ACE expression parsing/evaluation and C_PIN Set authorization already matched the cleaned sourced trajectories.
- Review note:
  - The first review packet included two `User1`-only BooleanExpr cases. They were removed and archived under `analysis/label_reviews/superseded/2026-05-24-cpin-user-ace-user1-only-ambiguity/` because reviewers correctly noted the source proves required support for `Admins` and `Admins OR UserMMMM`, not a universal ban on all vendor extensions.
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 5 retained labels with no concerns.
  - Reviewer files: `analysis/label_reviews/cpin_user_ace_clean_{a,b,c}.jsonl`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 655 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag cpin-user-ace-booleanexpr-doc`: 5 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 865 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 865 cases, 2730 review rows, 853 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 853 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl .venv/bin/python evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.

## 2026-05-24 21:02 KST - Locking Feature descriptor and hasLockedRange state batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - High for hidden Locking state dashboard traces. This closes a real permissive gap: before this repair, `hasLockedRange()` was treated as a generic host helper, so a trace could report `False` even after an enabled locked range and still pass. Raw Level 0 Locking Feature descriptor snapshots were likewise not checked against reconstructed Locking/MBR state.
- Trigger:
  - `opal/3.1.1.3` defines the Level 0 Locking Feature descriptor bits, including `MBR Done`, `MBR Enabled`, `Locked`, and `Locking Enabled`.
  - `opal/3.1.1.3.1` defines `LockingEnabled` as one when a Locking-template SP is not Nonexistent or Manufactured-Inactive.
  - `core/3.3.6.5.3` defines `Locked` as one only when LockingEnabled is one and at least one LBA range has enabled ReadLocked or enabled WriteLocked state.
  - `core/3.3.6.5.5` and `core/3.3.6.5.6` derive `MBREnabled`/`MBRDone` from LockingEnabled plus MBRControl Enable/Done.
- Result:
  - Added 10 accepted `locking-feature-descriptor-doc` cases.
  - Cases cover fresh Manufactured-Inactive disabled descriptor state, activated-but-unlocked state, enabled write/read locked range state, disabled lock feature not counting as Locked, and MBRControl Enable/Done driving MBR descriptor bits.
  - Sourced corpus is now 875 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 863 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Added `_locking_feature_enabled`, `_locking_feature_locked`, and `_expected_level0_locking_feature` in `src/solver_components/expectations.py`.
  - `hasLockedRange` now compares the returned boolean/status to the reconstructed official `Locked` predicate.
  - Raw `Level0Discovery` Locking Feature responses now validate `LockingEnabled`, `Locked`, `MBREnabled`, and `MBRDone` return fields.
  - `compare_expected_actual` now applies return-value validators to success-like host responses where status is `None`/`PASS`, not only literal `SUCCESS`.
  - Added 6 unit regressions in `tests/test_solver_rules.py`.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 10 labels with no concerns.
  - Reviewer files: `analysis/label_reviews/locking_feature_descriptor_{a,b,c}.jsonl`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 661 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag locking-feature-descriptor-doc`: 10 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 875 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 875 cases, 2760 review rows, 863 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 863 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `DATASET_DIR=dataset LABEL_PATH=dataset/label.jsonl python3 evaluate.py`: `score=100.00`.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 875 cases, 395 covered docs, 310 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 395 covered, 264 pending A/B cartography.

## 2026-05-24 21:11 KST - Locking Feature lifecycle descriptor sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden traces that snapshot Level 0 Locking Feature state after RevertSP. This makes the prior descriptor repair cover longer lifecycle trajectories, not just immediate lock/MBR mutations.
- Trigger:
  - `opal/5.1.3.3` says successful Locking SP RevertSP returns the Admin SP SP-table row and the Locking SP itself to Original Factory State.
  - `opal/5.2.2.2.2` says successful Revert/RevertSP changes LifeCycleState to Original Factory State and disables template functionality if that state was Manufactured-Inactive.
  - `opal/5.2.2.3.1` says Manufactured-Inactive SPs cannot open sessions and Locking SP locking/media-encryption management is disabled.
  - `opal/3.1.1.3.1` says LockingEnabled is zero when the Locking-template SP is Nonexistent or Manufactured-Inactive.
  - `core/3.3.6.5.3` through `core/3.3.6.5.6` define `Locked`, `MBREnabled`, and `MBRDone` descriptor formulas.
- Result:
  - Added 6 accepted `locking-feature-lifecycle-doc` cases.
  - Cases cover normal RevertSP and KeepGlobalRangeKey-success returning descriptor bits to disabled/unlocked, plus failed KeepGlobalRangeKey RevertSP preserving enabled/locked state.
  - Sourced corpus is now 881 cases; consensus accepted corpus is now 869 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - No additional production solver change was required. The previous descriptor-state repair and existing `_reset_locking_sp` lifecycle transition already matched these longer trajectories.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 6 labels with no concerns.
  - Reviewer files: `analysis/label_reviews/locking_feature_lifecycle_{a,b,c}.jsonl`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 661 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag locking-feature-lifecycle-doc`: 6 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 881 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 881 cases, 2778 review rows, 869 accepted, 12 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 869 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.

## 2026-05-24 22:33 KST - Data Removal, reset observability, DataStore cross-authority, and exact GetACL catch-up

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repairs + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - High for hidden AccessControl/GetACL traces. Before the latest repair, a `GetACL` response could return any uidref list of the right length and still pass; now known Opal preconfigured rows require the exact expected ACE uidrefs.
  - Medium/high for DataStore and reset-state hidden traces. The new cases stress longer cross-authority DataStore writes and post-reset observable MBR/Locking descriptor state rather than one-step method responses.
- Result:
  - Added and accepted 12 `data-removal-feature-doc` cases.
  - Added and accepted 8 `meta-acl-preconfig-empty-doc` cases.
  - Added and accepted 8 `locking-feature-reset-doc` cases.
  - Added and accepted 8 `locking-feature-mbr-reset-doc` cases.
  - Added and accepted 8 `mbrcontrol-reset-get-doc` cases.
  - Added and accepted 8 `datastore-cross-authority-doc` cases.
  - Added and accepted 12 `getacl-exact-acl-doc` cases.
  - Current sourced corpus is 945 cases; consensus-accepted corpus is 933 cases; quarantined corpus remains 12 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - `src/solver_components/models.py`: added `ExpectedResponse.expected_return_bit_masks` for descriptor bit assertions and `ExpectedResponse.expected_return_uid_refs` for exact UID/ACE-list assertions.
  - `src/solver_components/engine.py`: added bit-mask comparison, exact returned uidref extraction, ACE symbolic/UID canonicalization, and exact expected-ACE subset validation.
  - `src/solver_components/expectations.py`: added Data Removal Feature descriptor expectations, broadened known preconfigured meta-ACL handling, and added `_known_acl_return_refs` for known Locking SP AccessControl associations.
  - `tests/test_solver_rules.py`: added regressions for Data Removal descriptor validation and exact `GetACL` ACE-list behavior.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted `datastore-cross-authority-doc` with no concerns.
  - Three independent reviewers accepted `getacl-exact-acl-doc` with no concerns after one superseded round.
  - The first `getacl-exact-acl-doc` packet was archived under `analysis/label_reviews/superseded/2026-05-24-getacl-exact-acl-order-refresh/` because a reviewer flagged possible ACE-list order ambiguity. The regenerated accepted packet returns `MBRControl.Set` ACEs in the Opal table order.
  - The first `locking-feature-mbr-reset-doc` packet was archived under `analysis/label_reviews/superseded/2026-05-24-locking-feature-mbr-reset-evidence-refresh/` until explicit Programmatic/TPer reset and reset mapping evidence was included.

## 2026-05-24 22:45 KST - AddACE ACL state tracking sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + solver repair + independent review + quarantine handling + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium/high for hidden AccessControl traces. This closes a statefulness gap: before the repair, a successful `AddACE` did not mutate reconstructed ACL state, so a second identical `AddACE` could be accepted even though Core says adding an already-present ACE fails.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.3.14` says `AddACE` adds an ACE object uidref to the target AccessControl association's ACL column.
  - `core/5.3.3.14.4.1` says successful `AddACE` returns an empty list.
  - `core/5.3.3.14.5` says `AddACE` fails if the ACE does not exist, already exists in the ACL, the ACL is full, or `AddACEACL` is not authenticated.
  - `opal/4.2.1.5` gives the Admin SP `SPInfo/Get` row: ACL `ACE_Anybody`, `AddACEACL=ACE_Anybody`, and empty `GetACLACL`.
  - `opal/4.2.1.6` gives concrete Admin SP ACE rows including `ACE_Anybody` and `ACE_Admin`.
- Result:
  - Added 5 `addace-acl-state-doc` cases.
  - 4 cases entered the trusted consensus corpus:
    - first `SPInfo/Get AddACE(ACE_Admin)` can succeed with an empty result;
    - adding preexisting `ACE_Anybody` is not a valid success;
    - a second `AddACE(ACE_Admin)` after a prior successful add is not a valid success;
    - the duplicate check works across symbolic and numeric ACE uidref spellings.
  - 1 case was quarantined: `AddACE` using arbitrary UID `0000000800FFFFFF` as a nonexistent ACE. Two reviewers noted that the evidence proves the cited preconfigured ACE rows but does not universally exclude every possible vendor extension ACE row.
  - Current sourced corpus is 950 cases; consensus-accepted corpus is 937 cases; quarantined corpus is 13 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - `src/solver_components/models.py`: added dynamic AccessControl ACL addition/removal state.
  - `src/solver_components/semantics.py`: added ACE uidref canonicalization and ACE-existence helper.
  - `src/solver_components/transitions.py`: successful `AddACE`/`RemoveACE`/`SetACL` now updates reconstructed ACL state.
  - `src/solver_components/expectations.py`: `AddACE` now rejects duplicate ACE refs already present in the target ACL and rejects ACE refs that cannot be resolved as known issued ACEs.
  - `tests/test_solver_rules.py`: added three AddACE state regressions.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers reviewed `analysis/label_reviews/addace_acl_state_{a,b,c}.todo.jsonl`.
  - Four labels passed with no concerns and are accepted.
  - The arbitrary nonexistent-ACE case is deliberately quarantined in `analysis/quarantined_sourced_cases.json`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 674 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag addace-acl-state-doc`: 5 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 950 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 950 cases, 2985 review rows, 937 accepted, 13 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 937 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 950 cases, 400 covered docs, 306 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 400 covered, 260 pending A/B cartography.
  - `python3 tools/build_spec_graph.py`: 1376 sections, 1008 entities, 27 rules, 7863 edges, 950 test links.

## 2026-05-24 22:51 KST - Locking host-I/O impossible-success quarantine rework

- Change type: quarantine rework + sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden host I/O locking traces. This keeps the useful part of earlier quarantined locking cases while avoiding an unsupported exact status-code claim.
- Trigger:
  - Earlier `locking-doc` cases were quarantined because they used `INVALID_PARAMETER` as the concrete status for Data Protection Error.
  - `core/5.7.3.2` defines locked read/write behavior as Data Protection Error but does not map that behavior to one exact TCGstorageAPI status code.
  - `core/5.7.2.2.6` through `core/5.7.2.2.9` define when ReadLocked/WriteLocked are meaningful.
  - `core/5.7.2.5.2` and `core/5.7.2.5.3` define MBRControl Enable/Done so the regenerated trajectories explicitly set MBR shadowing inactive.
- Result:
  - Added 4 `locking-host-io-impossible-doc` cases.
  - All 4 passed three-reviewer consensus with no concerns.
  - Cases cover MBR-disabled enabled `ReadLocked`, mixed read-lock boundary crossing, enabled `WriteLocked`, and mixed write-lock boundary crossing as impossible `SUCCESS` responses.
  - Current sourced corpus is 954 cases; consensus-accepted corpus is 941 cases; quarantined corpus remains 13 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - No production solver change was required. Existing `_expected_host_io` already rejected successful host reads/writes against enabled locked ranges; this batch reworked the evidence/assertion shape.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 4 labels with no concerns.
  - Reviewer files: `analysis/label_reviews/locking_host_io_impossible_{a,b,c}.jsonl`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 674 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag locking-host-io-impossible-doc`: 4 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 954 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 954 cases, 2997 review rows, 941 accepted, 13 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 941 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 954 cases, 400 covered docs, 306 untriaged A/B.

## 2026-05-24 23:02 KST - GenKey ReEncryptState impossible-success quarantine rework

- Change type: quarantine rework + sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden re-encryption/media-key traces. This reinforces a high-priority Locking state-machine rule without depending on an exact error-code mapping.
- Trigger:
  - Earlier `genkey-doc` ReEncryptState cases were quarantined because the packet used raw `Locking` column `12` and numeric value `2`; reviewers had to infer that this meant `ReEncryptState=PENDING`.
  - `core/5.7.2.2.12` says `NextKey` and the referenced media encryption key object are writable only when `ReEncryptState` is `IDLE`; otherwise `GenKey` on the associated credential object returns an error.
  - `core/5.7.2.2.13` defines `ReEncryptState`.
  - `core/5.7.3.7.1` names the non-IDLE states: `PENDING`, `ACTIVE`, `COMPLETED`, and `PAUSED`.
  - `opal/4.3.5.5` ties K_AES key-table `Key` material to the `GenKey` method via `*K1`.
- Result:
  - Added 5 `genkey-reencrypt-state-doc` cases.
  - All 5 entered the trusted consensus corpus.
  - Cases cover `K_AES_256_Range1_Key.GenKey` with `ReEncryptState` observed as `PENDING`, `ACTIVE`, `COMPLETED`, and `PAUSED`, plus `K_AES_256_GlobalRange_Key.GenKey` with global `PENDING`.
  - Each case asserts only that a final `SUCCESS` response is impossible.
  - Current sourced corpus is 959 cases; consensus-accepted corpus is 946 cases; quarantined corpus remains 13 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - No production solver change was required. Existing `_expected_genkey` already blocks K_AES range-key `GenKey` when the associated range's `ReEncryptState` is not `IDLE`; this batch repaired the evidence/trajectory shape of earlier quarantined cases.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 5 labels with no concerns.
  - Reviewer files: `analysis/label_reviews/genkey_reencrypt_state_{a,b,c}.jsonl`.
  - The older `genkey-doc` quarantined cases remain historical and are not silently promoted.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 674 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag genkey-reencrypt-state-doc`: 5 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 959 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 959 cases, 3012 review rows, 946 accepted, 13 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 946 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 959 cases, 400 covered docs, 306 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 400 covered, 260 pending A/B cartography.
  - `python3 tools/build_spec_graph.py`: 1376 sections, 1017 entities, 27 rules, 7915 edges, 959 test links.

## 2026-05-24 23:08 KST - DataStore long trajectory sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden DataStore byte-table traces. This adds longer, more compositional tests around the area that previously looked score-relevant: byte offsets, partial overwrites, separate Get/Set ACLs, and failed-write non-mutation.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.3.7.1.2` says byte-table `Set` starts at `Where.Row`, or at row zero when `Where` is omitted.
  - `core/5.3.3.7.2.1` says byte-table `Set` writes `Bytes`.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.2` says unauthorized byte-table `Get` returns a `SUCCESS` response with an empty results list.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.6` says a `Set` whose access control does not permit attempted changes fails, and all requested changes fail as a whole.
  - `opal/4.3.1.6`, `opal/4.3.1.7`, and `opal/4.3.8.1` define DataStore Get/Set ACLs as separate, Admins-initial, and personalizable.
- Result:
  - Added 6 `datastore-long-trajectory-doc` cases.
  - All 6 entered the trusted consensus corpus.
  - The cases cover:
    - Admin writes a base payload, Set-only User1 partially overwrites bytes, User1 cannot read, Admin partially overwrites another region, and later Admin subrange Get must reflect both overwrites.
    - Later personalization of the DataStore Get ACE lets User1 read the current interleaved payload.
    - A User1 `Set` attempt without the Set ACE returns `NOT_AUTHORIZED` and must not mutate bytes observed by a later Admin Get.
  - Current sourced corpus is 965 cases; consensus-accepted corpus is 952 cases; quarantined corpus remains 13 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - No production solver change was required. Existing DataStore offset/payload state tracking and ACE authorization logic passed the longer trajectories.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 6 labels with no concerns.
  - Reviewer files: `analysis/label_reviews/datastore_long_{a,b,c}.jsonl`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 674 passed.

## 2026-05-24 23:21 KST - Created table GetSetACL meta-ACL sourced batch

- Change type: solver fix + sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium-high for hidden AccessControl/CreateTable traces. This is in the same score-relevant family as `AccessControl`/`GetACL`: newly created table method associations must exist, and their meta-ACL authorization comes from the `GetSetACL` supplied to `CreateTable`.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.4.2.1` says `CreateTable` creates AccessControl rows for methods available on the new table, including `TableUID.Next`, `TableUID.Get`, `TableUID.Set`, and object descriptor `Get` rows.
  - `core/5.3.3.2.3` says the `GetSetACL` list is placed into `AddACEACL`, `RemoveACEACL`, `GetACLACL`, and `DeleteMethodACL` for those AccessControl rows.
  - `core/5.3.4.3.1` defines the meta-ACL methods against the AccessControl row's `AddACEACL`, `RemoveACEACL`, `GetACLACL`, and `DeleteMethodACL`.
  - `core/5.1.5.2` says missing AccessControl rows or unsatisfied meta-ACLs return `NOT_AUTHORIZED`.
  - `core/5.3.3.11` says successful `DeleteMethod` removes the AccessControl association and returns an empty list.
- Result:
  - Added 6 `created-table-meta-acl-doc` cases.
  - All 6 entered the trusted consensus corpus.
  - Cases cover `CreateTable` with `GetSetACL=ACE_Anybody`, empty `GetSetACL`, successful `GetACL`/`DeleteMethod` authorization through `GetSetACL`, and post-`DeleteMethod` `GetACL` failure because the AccessControl association was removed.
  - Current sourced corpus is 971 cases; consensus-accepted corpus is 958 cases; quarantined corpus remains 13 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Added `State.created_table_getset_acls` to track the `GetSetACL` captured from successful `CreateTable`.
  - `transitions._apply_create_table_success` now canonicalizes and stores `GetSetACL` for newly created tables.
  - Reset/Revert cleanup now removes created-table `GetSetACL` state together with created table rows.
  - `semantics._combo_exists_for_get_acl` now recognizes AccessControl associations created by `CreateTable` for the new table UID and descriptor UID.
  - `expectations._known_meta_acl_authorization` now authorizes or rejects created-table meta-methods using the stored `GetSetACL` rather than treating them as unknown.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 6 labels with no concerns.
  - Reviewer files: `analysis/label_reviews/created_table_meta_acl_{a,b,c}.jsonl`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 676 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag created-table-meta-acl-doc`: 6 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 971 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 971 cases, 3048 review rows, 958 accepted, 13 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 958 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.

## 2026-05-24 23:31 KST - Created table deletion removes AccessControl associations

- Change type: solver fix + sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden AccessControl/CreateTable lifecycle traces. This extends the previous created-table GetSetACL repair into table deletion: after a table descriptor is deleted, the solver must stop accepting stale `GetACL` results for the deleted table or its descriptor.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.4.2.1` says `CreateTable` creates a Table descriptor object and AccessControl rows for the new table and descriptor.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.4` says deleting a Table table row has the side effect of deleting the table with which that descriptor object is associated.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.5` says table deletion deletes the table descriptor, the table itself, all associated AccessControl rows for both the table and descriptor, and all associated Column rows.
  - `core/5.1.5.2` says `GetACL` returns `NOT_AUTHORIZED` when no AccessControl row represents the requested InvokingID/MethodID combination.
- Result:
  - Added 5 `created-table-delete-acl-doc` cases.
  - 4 entered the trusted consensus corpus.
  - Trusted cases cover post-`DeleteRow` `GetACL` on both the deleted table UID and deleted descriptor UID: `NOT_AUTHORIZED` is accepted, stale `SUCCESS` with an ACL is rejected.
  - 1 case was quarantined: `created-table-delete-acl-doc-e1be4004ed`, which asserted that deleting the table descriptor releases the same `NewTableName`/`CommonName` for a later `CreateTable`. Reviewers agreed with the label but recorded concerns because the packet required an inference about name reuse after deletion rather than an explicit reuse-policy statement.
  - Current sourced corpus is 976 cases; consensus-accepted corpus is 962 cases; quarantined corpus is 14 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - Added `State.created_table_name_by_uid` so cleanup can connect created table UIDs back to their recorded name/common-name reservation.
  - Added `transitions._remove_created_table_state` to remove created table state as a coherent lifecycle object: table row state, row UID state, descriptor mapping, size/allocation state, stored `GetSetACL`, dynamic ACL additions/removals, deleted-method tombstones, and name reservation.
  - `transitions._apply_delete_row_success` now detects successful `DeleteRow` on the Table table descriptor for a created table and invokes the created-table cleanup path.
  - Locking SP reset and factory reset reuse the cleanup path/clear the new name map.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers labeled all 5 cases with the intended PASS/FAIL distribution.
  - Reviewer files: `analysis/label_reviews/created_table_delete_acl_{a,b,c}.jsonl`.
  - The name-reuse case is held out of `analysis/accepted_sourced_cases.json` because two reviewers recorded concerns. It remains in quarantine for possible rework with stronger uniqueness/reuse evidence.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 678 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag created-table-delete-acl-doc`: 5 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 976 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 976 cases, 3063 review rows, 962 accepted, 14 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 962 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 976 cases, 401 covered docs, 305 untriaged A/B.
  - `python3 tools/build_doc_inventory.py`: 1376 rows, 401 covered, 259 pending A/B cartography.
  - `python3 tools/build_spec_graph.py`: 1376 sections, 1034 entities, 27 rules, 8081 edges, 976 test links.

## 2026-05-24 23:39 KST - Created table descriptor Delete object path

- Change type: solver fix + sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium for hidden table lifecycle traces. This closes the sibling path to the previous `DeleteRow` repair: the Core spec permits table deletion through either `DeleteRow` on the Table table row or `Delete` on the descriptor object itself.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.3.3.3` says the Delete object method deletes the invoked object.
  - `core/5.3.3.3.1.1` says successful Delete returns an empty list and otherwise fails atomically.
  - `core/5.3.4.2.5` says a table is deleted by successful `Delete` or `DeleteRow` on the associated Table descriptor object, and that deletion removes all associated AccessControl rows for both the table and descriptor.
  - `core/5.1.5.2` says later `GetACL` must return `NOT_AUTHORIZED` when the AccessControl association no longer exists.
- Result:
  - Added 4 `created-table-delete-object-doc` cases.
  - All 4 entered the trusted consensus corpus.
  - Cases cover descriptor-object `Delete` success with empty result, rejection of unexpected Delete return payload, post-Delete stale table `GetACL` rejection, and post-Delete stale descriptor `GetACL` rejection.
  - Current sourced corpus is 980 cases; consensus-accepted corpus is 966 cases; quarantined corpus remains 14 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - `expectations._expected_delete` now recognizes created Table descriptor objects as valid Delete targets rather than treating Delete as only a Locking-range operation.
  - `transitions._apply_delete_success` now routes successful descriptor-object Delete through the same created-table cleanup path used by descriptor `DeleteRow`.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 4 labels with no concerns.
  - Reviewer files: `analysis/label_reviews/created_table_delete_object_{a,b,c}.jsonl`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 680 passed.

## 2026-05-24 23:49 KST - Locking reset long trajectory sourced batch

- Change type: sourced cases + independent review + regression verification
- Dashboard impact estimate:
  - Medium-high for hidden Locking state-machine traces. This expands the score-relevant LockOnReset area into longer, mixed trajectories involving reset matching, manual lock-cell clearing, disabled lock-enable columns, host I/O, and Level 0 Locked observation.
- Trigger:
  - `core/5.7.2.2.10` says matching LockOnReset reset types set `ReadLocked` and `WriteLocked` to True, while an empty set leaves them unchanged.
  - `core/5.7.2.2.6` and `core/5.7.2.2.7` say disabled read/write lock-enable columns make the corresponding locked cell disregarded/ignored.
  - `core/3.3.6.5.3` says the Locking Feature Locked bit is set only when an enabled read or write lock is active.
  - `core/3.3.7.1.5`, `opal/3.2.3`, `opal/3.3.5.2`, and `opal/4.2.3.1` provide reset/session-abort and Programmatic reset behavior.
- Result:
  - Added 10 `locking-reset-long-trajectory-doc` cases.
  - All 10 entered the trusted consensus corpus.
  - Cases cover:
    - PowerCycle locking a write-enabled range, then disabling `WriteLockEnabled` so host write succeeds and Level 0 Locked clears despite stored `WriteLocked=True`.
    - PowerCycle locking `{0,3}`, manual clearing of `ReadLocked`/`WriteLocked`, nonmatching HardwareReset preserving the cleared cells, then enabled TPER_RESET relocking them.
    - Split read/write behavior where PowerCycle read-locks an enabled read side while a disabled write side remains ignored, so host write succeeds but host read cannot report success.
  - Current sourced corpus is 990 cases; consensus-accepted corpus is 976 cases; quarantined corpus remains 14 cases.
- Solver changes:
  - No production solver change was required. Existing Locking reset state, enable-column, host I/O, and Level 0 descriptor logic passed the longer accepted trajectories.
- Review note:
  - Three independent reviewers accepted all 10 labels with no concerns.
  - Reviewer files: `analysis/label_reviews/locking_reset_long_{a,b,c}.jsonl`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_solver_rules.py -q`: 680 passed.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --tag locking-reset-long-trajectory-doc`: 10 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py`: 990 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/label_consensus.py report`: 990 cases, 3105 review rows, 976 accepted, 14 quarantined.
  - `python3 tools/run_sourced_edges.py --consensus-gate`: 976 accepted cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/run_synthetic_edges.py`: 205 cases, 0 mismatches.
  - `python3 tools/doc_coverage.py`: 1376 docs, 990 cases, 401 covered docs, 305 untriaged A/B.
